message:
test(frankenpandas): lock in README Quick Example via integration test (br-we6ql / fd90.146)

fd90.146: regression guard for the fd90.121-fd90.144 prelude expansion.
The 6 unit tests inside src/lib.rs exercised individual prelude items;
this new integration test runs the README's Quick Example AND Quick
Start end-to-end through the prelude.

If the README evolves and the prelude no longer covers the documented
example, this test fails — instead of users hitting compile errors.
Equivalently: if someone removes a prelude item this depends on, the
fail surfaces in CI before the README looks broken.

Two test cases in crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs:

  readme_quick_example_compiles_and_runs (Quick Example, lines 41-63):
    read_csv_str, query, groupby, agg_named,
    write_json_string + JsonOrient, write_feather_bytes

  readme_quick_start_round_trip_through_sqlite (Quick Start, lines 209-234):
    + Series::from_values, IndexLabel/Scalar via prelude, to_datetime,
    + write_csv_string, write_sql, read_sql_table, SqlIfExists,
    + frankenpandas::rusqlite::Connection (sql-sqlite feature; gated)

Verified:
- cargo test -p frankenpandas:
    6 lib tests pass (existing prelude coverage)
    2 integration tests pass (new)
    2 ignored doctests (rustdoc examples; pre-existing)

Pure additive test. No behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Verify cargo doc cleanly under RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:20:27.880362095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:22:33.296973084Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:22:33.294410403Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","review"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-we6ql","title":"fd90.146: lock in README Quick Example via integration test (regression guard for prelude)","description":"fd90.74-145 README audit. The fd90.121-fd90.144 sequence expanded the frankenpandas prelude to cover the README's documented API surface. The 6 unit tests inside src/lib.rs (prelude_smoke_test, prelude_concat_works, prelude_query_works, prelude_io_roundtrip, prelude_to_datetime_works, rusqlite_reexport_quickstart_compiles) exercise individual prelude items but not the END-TO-END Quick Example as a single script.

Without a single integration test that runs the README's Quick Example top-to-bottom, future prelude changes (or accidental removals) wouldn't surface a regression until users complain.

Fix: add crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (an integration test, NOT in src/lib.rs) that mirrors the README's Quick Example block at lines 41-63, plus the more elaborate Quick Start at lines 209-234, exercising:
  read_csv_str, query, groupby, agg_named,
  write_json_string, write_feather_bytes,
  Series::from_values, to_datetime,
  write_csv_string, write_sql, read_sql_table, SqlIfExists,
  frankenpandas::rusqlite::Connection::open_in_memory.

Verify with cargo test -p frankenpandas (default features, sql-sqlite enabled). The test must compile only via 'use frankenpandas::prelude::*' to lock in prelude completeness.

If the README example evolves, the test must too — or the README is lying.

Pure additive test. No behavior change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:55:16.374371595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:58:05.930614166Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:58:05.927845775Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-weh1","title":"[LOW] community: no .github/FUNDING.yml — no Sponsor button / funding channels declared","description":"GitHub Sponsors / funding configuration audit.

**Observation.** `ls .github/FUNDING.yml` → No such file. No declared funding channels (GitHub Sponsors / Open Collective / Liberapay / etc.).

**Context.** For an open-source library targeting a sizable user base (pandas has tens of millions of users; frankenpandas targets their Rust-curious subset), a documented funding mechanism is a cheap way to let engaged users contribute financially. GitHub renders a "Sponsor" button on the repo header when `FUNDING.yml` is present; without it, the button is absent and engaged users have no path.

**Severity.** LOW — no operational impact. No release blocker. No code quality issue. Filing truthfully as LOW; user-visible polish, optional feature.

**Fix shape.** `.github/FUNDING.yml`:
```yaml
# Map the repo's "Sponsor" button to one or more funding platforms.
# Each line enables a platform. Remove entries that don't apply.

github: [<org-or-user>]           # GitHub Sponsors
# open_collective: frankenpandas
# liberapay: <name>
# ko_fi: <name>
# custom: ["https://frankenpandas.dev/sponsor"]
```

Requires a decision: does the maintainer have an active GitHub Sponsors account? If yes, one-line file. If not, skip this bead entirely.

Blast radius: 1 tiny file (< 10 lines), zero code changes.

Prerequisites: Maintainer decides which funding channel(s) to expose. Nothing else.

Related:
- br-8k1i (SECURITY.md), br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — same .github/ polish tier.
- No interlocks with other beads.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:18:55.853913193Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:58.138001Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:58.138001Z","close_reason":".github/FUNDING.yml already shipped on main.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["community","funding","review"]}

Two more stale numeric/factual claims: (1) line 125 says fp-frame has 788 methods - actual count of pub fn lines in fp-frame/src/lib.rs is 964. (2) Lines 37 and 869 claim conformance is all currently green under cargo test -p fp-conformance - actually 5 pre-existing tests fail (live_oracle_unavailable_falls_back_to_fixture_when_enabled, packet_filter_runs_csv_read_frame_parse_dates_mixed_timezone_packet, packet_filter_runs_dataframe_concat_axis1_packet, packet_filter_runs_dataframe_to_json_records_packet, fuzz_seed_fixtures_fuzz_json_io_bytes_accepts_records_seed_fixture). Update method count to current; soften all green claim to mention the small known-failing set so users running cargo test see expected behavior. Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:21:46.528434217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:24:11.077891267Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:24:11.075275966Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","readme","review"]}

@@ -0,0 +1,82 @@
+//! Integration test: README Quick Example + Quick Start
+//!
+//! Locks in that the documented prelude is sufficient to compile and run the
+//! examples shown in the top-level README. If the README evolves, this test
+//! must evolve too — or the README is lying.
+//!
+//! Tracks fd90.146 (br-frankenpandas-we6ql). Regression guard for fd90.121
+//! through fd90.144 prelude expansion.
+
+use frankenpandas::prelude::*;
+
+/// README Quick Example (lines 41-63).
+///
+/// Imports prelude only. Verifies:
+/// - read_csv_str
+/// - DataFrame.query (DataFrameExprExt trait method)
+/// - DataFrame.groupby + DataFrameGroupBy.agg_named
+/// - write_json_string + JsonOrient
+/// - write_feather_bytes
+#[test]
+fn readme_quick_example_compiles_and_runs() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
+    let df = read_csv_str("name,age,city\nAlice,30,NYC\nBob,25,LA\nCarol,35,NYC")?;
+
+    let adults = df.query("age > 28")?;
+
+    let summary = adults.groupby(&["city"])?.agg_named(&[
+        ("avg_age", "age", "mean"),
+        ("count", "age", "count"),
+    ])?;
+
+    let _json = write_json_string(&summary, JsonOrient::Records)?;
+    let _feather = write_feather_bytes(&summary)?;
+
+    // Sanity-check: only NYC group survives the query filter
+    // (Alice 30 + Carol 35; LA's Bob is filtered out at age > 28).
+    assert_eq!(summary.index().len(), 1);
+    Ok(())
+}
+
+/// README Quick Start (lines 209-234).
+///
+/// Imports prelude only. Verifies broader API surface including:
+/// - Series::from_values + IndexLabel/Scalar via prelude
+/// - to_datetime
+/// - write_csv_string
+/// - frankenpandas::rusqlite::Connection (sql-sqlite feature, on by default)
+/// - write_sql + SqlIfExists
+/// - read_sql_table
+#[cfg(feature = "sql-sqlite")]
+#[test]
+fn readme_quick_start_round_trip_through_sqlite() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
+    let df = read_csv_str(
+        "ticker,price,volume\nAAPL,185.50,1000\nGOOG,140.25,500\nAAPL,186.00,1200",
+    )?;
+
+    let expensive = df.query("price > 150")?;
+    let by_ticker = expensive.groupby(&["ticker"])?.sum()?;
+
+    // Series construction via prelude — exercises Series + IndexLabel + Scalar.
+    let dates = Series::from_values(
+        "d",
+        vec![0i64.into()],
+        vec![Scalar::Utf8("2024-01-15".into())],
+    )?;
+    let _parsed = to_datetime(&dates)?;
+
+    // Format exports.
+    let _csv = write_csv_string(&by_ticker)?;
+    let _json = write_json_string(&by_ticker, JsonOrient::Records)?;
+    let _feather = write_feather_bytes(&by_ticker)?;
+
+    // SQL round-trip via the re-exported rusqlite (fd90.130).
+    let conn = frankenpandas::rusqlite::Connection::open_in_memory()?;
+    write_sql(&by_ticker, &conn, "results", SqlIfExists::Fail)?;
+    let back = read_sql_table(&conn, "results")?;
+
+    // Both AAPL trades survive price > 150 filter; GOOG (140.25) is dropped.
+    // After groupby(ticker).sum(), only the AAPL group row remains.
+    assert_eq!(by_ticker.index().len(), 1);
+    assert_eq!(back.index().len(), 1);
+    Ok(())
+}
